notes: Singh et al. 2023, Science: taurine deficiency as a driver of aging; taurine declines ~3x from youth to old age across species; supplementation extended mouse median lifespan 10-12%; monkey data showed improved glucose, bone density, immune markers

2025 Aging Cell rebuttal: experimental evidence against taurine deficiency as aging driver in humans; argues decline may be a biomarker not a cause; confounders not excluded; human longevity RCT evidence absent
Examine.com overview: cardiovascular, metabolic, and exercise performance evidence; blood pressure RCT meta-analysis data; bile acid conjugation mechanism; dosing and safety summary

Fernandez et al. 2025, Science (Vol 388): NIH longitudinal study (Baltimore Longitudinal Study of Aging) finds taurine INCREASES or remains UNCHANGED with age in humans, monkeys, and mice — directly contradicts cross-sectional 2023 finding; taurine unlikely to be a reliable aging biomarker; corrections to resource description and body applied
